verdict: Both iter2 blockers (B1, B2) are confirmed fixed and live-verified. B1: posttool Write branch now builds a 3-candidate task_id list [env_CLAUDE_TASK_ID or session_id, data.task_id, session_id] de-duped, symmetric with pretool write-guard.sh. Sentinel keyed by session_id is found and consumed even when data.task_id differs. B2: AC4 scope-integrity test now uses baseline SHA 1a1d058 and BASELINE_DIRTY exclusion — passes cleanly. All 44 tests pass (4 AC + 40 consolidation). All 6 adversarial probes pass. Zero TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels. Codex self-review (quota/interactive-mode fallback): no blocking issues found. One pre-existing out-of-scope observation noted: Bash posttool task_id priority order differs from Write branch (pre-existing, not introduced by this cycle). Verdict: PASS — ready for close.

checks.name: TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel scan — all 4 AC test files
checks.detail: Grepped all 4 files in tests/generated/20260522-080646-B/ for literal string 'TEST_INCOMPLETE:'. Zero matches found. All test bodies are real implementations.

checks.name: pytest tests/generated/20260522-080646-B/ — all 4 AC tests
checks.detail: 4/4 tests pass: test_AC1_AC1-write-sentinel-matcher.py PASS, test_AC2_AC2-write-guard-sentinel-check.py PASS, test_AC3_AC3-posttool-write-sentinel-consume.py PASS, test_AC4_AC4-scope-integrity.py PASS. Clean run, 0 failures. Output: '4 passed in 0.84s'.

checks.name: pytest hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py — full suite
checks.detail: 40/40 tests pass. All 5 TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite cases pass (match_returns_entry, session_mismatch_returns_none, target_mismatch_returns_none, expired_sentinel_returns_none, op_mismatch_returns_none). All existing test classes also pass. Output: '40 passed in 0.04s'.

checks.name: B1 fix: posttool Write branch 3-candidate list structure
checks.detail: posttool-allowlist-consume.py lines 148-156: env_task_id=os.environ.get('CLAUDE_TASK_ID',''); writer_primary = env_task_id if env_task_id else session_id; data_task_id = data.get('task_id') or ''; de-duped candidate list built via seen set iterating [writer_primary, data_task_id, session_id]. Lines 161-172: iterates candidates calling match_sentinel_grant_for_write(candidate, session_id, file_path); consumes on first match using consumed_task_id. Bash branch (lines 101-139) unchanged.

checks.name: B1 fix: live probe — session-keyed sentinel consumed when data.task_id differs
checks.detail: Sentinel keyed by session_id='test-probe-session-iter3', CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset, data.task_id='different-task-id-xyz'. Candidates=['test-probe-session-iter3', 'different-task-id-xyz']. match_sentinel_grant_for_write(session_id, session_id, '/tmp/test-target.py') returned entry on first candidate. consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result() called. Sentinel file verified deleted after consume. Confirms iter2 B1 bug is resolved: sentinel no longer persists across multiple Write events.

checks.name: B2 fix: AC4 uses baseline SHA 1a1d058 and BASELINE_DIRTY exclusion
checks.detail: test_AC4_AC4-scope-integrity.py: BASELINE_HEAD_SHA='1a1d058665a1f525b9e4c219c93f69ebb2ae90bb', BASELINE_DIRTY={'hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh'}. net_scope = all_changed - BASELINE_DIRTY. Forbidden files checked against net_scope (not all_changed). hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh removed from scope check via BASELINE_DIRTY subtraction. Test runs git diff --name-only BASELINE_HEAD_SHA (not HEAD). AC4 PASS confirmed live.

checks.name: Symmetry: posttool and pretool candidate lists are identical
checks.detail: Extracted candidate-list logic from both posttool (lines 148-156) and pretool write-guard.sh (lines 59-65). Ran algebraic equivalence test across 5 scenarios: (1) CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset+data.task_id set, (2) CLAUDE_TASK_ID==data.task_id, (3) CLAUDE_TASK_ID!=data.task_id, (4) all equal, (5) no env/no data.task_id. All 5 produce byte-identical candidate lists. Sentinel writer always finds first-priority candidate in both hooks.

checks.name: Cross-session consumption blocked — session_id guard in match_sentinel_grant_for_write
checks.detail: match_sentinel_grant_for_write(task_id, session_B, file_path) where sentinel keyed by task_id has session_id=session_A. Function checks grant.get('session_id') != session_id and returns None. Live probe: session_A sentinel not consumed by session_B posttool event. Session isolation holds.

checks.name: Cross-task probe: 3-candidate loop does not consume wrong task's sentinel
checks.detail: Two sentinels: A keyed by session_id (target=/tmp/file-A.py) and B keyed by data_task_id (target=/tmp/file-B.py). Write event for /tmp/file-B.py with data.task_id=task-B. Candidates=[session_id, task-B-id]. First candidate (session_id) checked: match_sentinel_grant_for_write returns None because sentinel A target=/tmp/file-A.py != /tmp/file-B.py. Second candidate (task-B-id) matches. Sentinel A remains intact. Correct task's sentinel consumed, wrong sentinel untouched.

checks.name: Malformed-grant fallback does not prematurely consume valid Bash-op sentinel
checks.detail: Sentinel with op='Bash' (not Write): match_sentinel_grant_for_write returns None (op mismatch). Malformed-grant fallback condition: _enumerate_sentinel_grant_files(candidate) returns files AND load_sentinel_grant_for_task(candidate) returns None. Since sentinel is valid JSON, load returns non-None. Fallback condition is False. Bash-op sentinel is NOT consumed by a mismatched Write posttool event. No cross-op sentinel consumption.

checks.name: Bash branch unchanged — no regression
checks.detail: Bash branch (lines 101-139): task_id = data.get('task_id') or CLAUDE_TASK_ID or session_id — identical to pre-iter3. The Write elif block (lines 140-190) is a separate branch that does not affect Bash resolution. Bash branch uses match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command (checks op='Bash'), which cannot match Write sentinels. Verified: Write sentinel remains intact after simulated Bash PostToolUse. No regression.

checks.name: AC4 baseline SHA validity
checks.detail: git cat-file -t 1a1d058665a1f525b9e4c219c93f69ebb2ae90bb → 'commit'. SHA is valid commit: '1a1d058 feat(dev-cycle): Cycle 2 remediation — test-writer infra + cleanliness + style'. Baseline SHA correctly identifies the task's starting point. Net scope check on live diff: 96 files in net_scope (97 all_changed - 1 BASELINE_DIRTY). All 3 required files present. No forbidden files in net_scope.

checks.name: BASELINE_DIRTY robustness analysis
checks.detail: BASELINE_DIRTY is task-scoped (only this test uses it). Risk if sibling changes committed: BASELINE_DIRTY subtraction is a no-op (harmless set difference with non-member). Risk if new unauthorized files appear: they correctly show as scope violations. Risk of false negatives: none — BASELINE_DIRTY only excludes the one documented pre-dirty sibling file. Approach is robust for this task.

checks.name: Bash branch task_id priority vs Write branch — pre-existing asymmetry
checks.detail: Bash branch resolves task_id as [data.task_id, CLAUDE_TASK_ID, session_id] (single-lookup). Write branch uses 3-candidate list [CLAUDE_TASK_ID or session_id, data.task_id, session_id] (priority inverted). This asymmetry is pre-existing for Bash and is not introduced by iter3. The Bash task_id mismatch bug (when CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset and data.task_id != session_id) was documented as out-of-scope pre-existing issue in iter2 observations. Iter3 does not worsen it. Classified as out-of-scope observation.

observations.description: Bash posttool task_id priority order differs from Write branch (pre-existing asymmetry)
observations.detail: Bash posttool resolves task_id as data.task_id OR CLAUDE_TASK_ID OR session_id. Write branch uses [CLAUDE_TASK_ID or session_id, data.task_id, session_id]. When CLAUDE_TASK_ID is unset and data.task_id != session_id, Bash branch tries data.task_id first (may miss session-keyed sentinel). This is a pre-existing condition that predates this cycle and is not worsened by iter3. Flagged as observation per iter2 OBS-3 (pre-existing Bash single-candidate gap).

iter3_fixes_verified.B1_posttool_3candidate_lookup: PASS — Write branch builds [CLAUDE_TASK_ID or session_id, data.task_id, session_id] de-duped list; iterates candidates; consumes on first match; sentinel file deleted after live consume
iter3_fixes_verified.B2_AC4_baseline_sha_and_baseline_dirty: PASS — BASELINE_HEAD_SHA='1a1d058665a1f525b9e4c219c93f69ebb2ae90bb', BASELINE_DIRTY={'hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh'}, net_scope excludes pre-dirty sibling file; AC4 test PASS

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex CLI entered interactive mode (stdin tty mode) rather than processing the prompt as exec. Output was only the session header and 'Ready' message. No CODEX_FEEDBACK produced.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review substituted with 6 adversarial probes: (1) cross-task wrong-sentinel consumption — PASS, target specificity prevents it; (2) BASELINE_DIRTY robustness — PASS, task-scoped and handles commit/no-commit scenarios correctly; (3) malformed-grant fallback vs valid-but-wrong-op sentinel — PASS, fallback only fires when load returns None (malformed), not on valid-but-mismatched sentinels; (4) pretool/posttool candidate list symmetry — PASS, identical for all 5 test scenarios; (5) Bash branch regression — PASS, unchanged; pre-existing asymmetry noted as OBS-1; (6) AC4 baseline SHA validity — PASS, SHA is a valid reachable commit object.

qa.overall_assessment: Iter3 correctly fixes both iter2 blockers. B1 (posttool 3-candidate lookup) is implemented correctly and symmetrically with the pretool. B2 (AC4 provenance-aware scope test) is implemented correctly with baseline SHA and BASELINE_DIRTY exclusion. All 44 tests pass. Six adversarial probes found zero new issues. One pre-existing out-of-scope observation (Bash posttool priority asymmetry) documented as OBS-1. Ready for close.

success_criteria_results.criterion: AC1: match_sentinel_grant_for_write() exists in allowlist.py and matches/rejects correctly
success_criteria_results.evidence: Function at allowlist.py:484-524. 5 TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite unit tests all pass. AC1 test PASS. Entry-level match, session mismatch, target mismatch, expired, op mismatch all handled correctly.

success_criteria_results.criterion: AC2: pretool-write-guard.sh checks sentinel before legacy /allow grant
success_criteria_results.evidence: Sentinel check at write-guard.sh:41-96 before legacy /allow block at line 97. 3-candidate lookup in inline Python block. CF-1 invariant: valid_no_match + existing file -> exit 2. AC2 4/4 smoke cases PASS (verified in iter2, unchanged in iter3).

success_criteria_results.criterion: AC3: posttool-allowlist-consume.py consumes Write sentinel on terminal result
success_criteria_results.evidence: elif tool_name=='Write' branch at posttool:140-190. 3-candidate list applied. AC3 3/3 cases PASS. Live probe confirmed sentinel consumed and deleted. Bash event does NOT consume Write sentinel.

success_criteria_results.criterion: AC4: scope integrity — no files outside hooks/ modified
success_criteria_results.evidence: test_AC4 uses git diff --name-only 1a1d058 (baseline SHA, not HEAD) and subtracts BASELINE_DIRTY={'hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh'}. Net scope = 96 files. All 3 required files present. No forbidden files in net scope. Test PASS.

root_cause_verification.rationale: All three structural absences (missing match_sentinel_grant_for_write, missing sentinel check in write-guard.sh, posttool restricted to Bash-only) are filled. The iter2-introduced asymmetry (posttool single-candidate vs pretool 3-candidate) is now resolved. Both hooks use identical candidate-list logic. Single sentinel cannot authorize multiple Write events — live probe confirms sentinel consumed on first match.
